steps.title: Identify what needs demonstrating
steps.content: The claim that the narrator was frightened signals we need evidence of his emotional fear, not just observation of the sailor's behavior or appearance.

steps.title: Match the fear signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice D directly conveys fear through vivid nightmare imagery—the sailor “haunted” the narrator's dreams with “diabolical expressions,” showing raw emotional distress. Choices A, B, and C describe the sailor's arrival, his quiet demeanor, or his unusual appearance without expressing fear. That matches choice D — D is correct.
